cssExists.detail: PostHero.css exists at blocks/PostHero/PostHero.css. `.post-hero .top-gradient` rule confirmed bundled in public/css/screen.css at line 149. CSS is included via screen.css global stylesheet (no block-specific style registration needed — pattern used by this theme).

heroClassAbsent.detail: All 4 rendered block instances use `class="post-hero relative overflow-visible not-discourse wp-block-takt-post-hero"` — the `hero` class is NOT present on any wrapper. Confirmed via REST API rendered content and page_audit.

gradientRendersCorrectly.detail: Gradient renders correctly on all 4 instances. The `.top-gradient` div is present in every instance (page_audit extra_selector confirmed count=1 per section). Screenshots show gradient backgrounds rendering visually at all breakpoints (375, 768, 1024, 1440px). PostHero.css is scoped to `.post-hero .top-gradient` and correctly independent from Hero.css (which targets `.hero .top-gradient`).

resizeObserver.detail: PostHero.js ResizeObserver logic verified: querySelector targets `.post-hero .top-gradient` (scoped correctly), finds nearest `<main>` element, applies `maxHeight = main.offsetHeight`. No JS errors observed on page load. screen.js is loaded on the test page (confirmed in pageInfo.scripts).

noJsErrors.detail: Page loaded without 500 errors. JS files load correctly (screen.js present in page scripts list). No PHP fatal errors. Build and lint passed with 0 errors.

blockRendersCorrectly.detail: All 4 block instances render with correct content. Variations confirmed: (1) Default - page accent color, no excerpt; (2) With Excerpt shown - excerpt appears between title and author; (3) Topic accent color override - blue class on section; (4) Full options - excerpt + topic color. All show post title, author, published date. Featured image renders with srcset.

build.detail: Build passes with 0 errors. 10 lint warnings (react-hooks/exhaustive-deps) across various blocks — none in PostHero specifically except 2 pre-existing warnings in PostHero.tsx (lines 72-73, `useSelect` dependency).

registration.detail: block.json is valid: apiVersion 3, name `takt/post-hero`, required fields present. All attribute types valid. No child blocks. viewScript (PostHero.js) and render (PostHero.php) referenced correctly.

attributes.detail: showExcerpt (boolean, default false) and useTopicColor (boolean, default false) tested. Excerpt visible in variations 2 and 4 only (showExcerpt=true). Topic color (blue class) applied in variations 3 and 4 only (useTopicColor=true). anchor attribute supported via block supports.

edgeCases.detail: Page renders on a published post with real content. Featured image, categories, author, date all auto-populated from post data. Image uses srcset for responsive loading.

interactiveBehavior.detail: ResizeObserver JS attaches on DOMContentLoaded. Targets `.post-hero .top-gradient` elements, observes `<main>` for resize events, caps max-height dynamically. No interactive UI elements (no accordions, tabs, carousels).

accessibility.detail: Semantic section elements used. h1 for post title (correct — single post hero). Featured image has role=presentation and alt='' (decorative). Category pills use inline-flex spans. No interactive elements requiring keyboard/ARIA checks.

markupDuplication.detail: No duplicate markup patterns found. No CSS-only visibility toggles with duplicated content.

tsxPhpSync.detail: Automated sync check flagged a false-positive root element mismatch (TSX uses Fragment+InspectorControls wrapper, PHP uses section — expected for editor-only controls). However, a real discrepancy was found manually: TSX uses `hero-inner` class on the content wrapper div (line 124), while PHP correctly uses `post-hero-inner` (line 38). This is an editor-only class mismatch — the PHP (frontend) is correct. The editor preview uses the wrong class name, which could affect editor-specific CSS targeting.

specCompliance.detail: 4 block instances on test page (page ID 181, post type). All wrapped in demo-container. All expected variations present: default, with excerpt, with topic color, with both.

issues.description: TSX editor uses class `hero-inner` on the inner content wrapper (PostHero.tsx line 124), but PHP frontend template correctly uses `post-hero-inner` (PostHero.php line 38). The frontend is correct; the editor has the wrong class name. This does not affect frontend rendering but could affect editor-specific CSS targeting if any styles target `.hero-inner`.
issues.fix: Change `className="hero-inner relative"` to `className="post-hero-inner relative"` in PostHero.tsx to match the PHP template.
issues.alsoUpdate: No PHP changes needed — PostHero.php is already correct.

issues.description: Image loading attribute difference: TSX editor renders img without `loading="lazy"` (line 135), while PHP frontend uses `wp_get_attachment_image()` which automatically adds `loading="lazy"`. Not a functional issue but documents the expected divergence.
issues.fix: Acceptable divergence — editor preview image does not need lazy loading. Document as intentional.

passedChecks: PostHero.css exists and `.post-hero .top-gradient` rule is bundled in public/css/screen.css (line 149)
No `hero` class on PostHero wrapper — all instances use `post-hero` class only
Gradient renders correctly via scoped `.post-hero .top-gradient` rule, independent from Hero.css
PostHero.js ResizeObserver targets `.post-hero .top-gradient` correctly and caps max-height to `<main>`
No JS errors — page loads cleanly, screen.js present
Block renders correctly with all 4 variations (default, excerpt, topic color, both)
Attributes showExcerpt and useTopicColor work correctly

visualObservations.375px: Mobile layout renders correctly. Hero occupies most of the viewport height. Post title is large and readable in charcoal. Featured image visible with gradient overlay. Category pill (Student Life) shown. Author and date below title. Excerpt visible in variation 2 and 4. Gradient background color changes correctly between page accent (neon-green) and topic accent (blue) variations.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet layout shows wider hero with improved proportions. Title text scales up. All 4 variations clearly distinct — default (neon-green gradient), with excerpt (text block visible), topic color (blue gradient), and full options. DemoContainer labels visible between sections.
visualObservations.1024px: Laptop width — hero takes good proportions. Image mask gradient works correctly. Content column constrained to max-w-[700px] mr-auto as expected. All variations render correctly.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop — all 4 variations clearly visible. Gradient system fully visible with smooth color transitions from accent color at top to off-white. Image mask gradient blends image into gradient smoothly. Topic color override (blue) clearly distinguishable from page accent color (neon-green) in variations 3 and 4. Related posts section and footer visible below blocks.

contentComparison.note: Content comparison skipped — test page is a post (type=post), not a page, and compare tools require slug-based page lookup. Block count verified via REST API post endpoint (ID 181). 4 takt/post-hero instances confirmed in rendered HTML.
